records.ety: [OE. þreschen, þreshen, to beat, AS. þerscan, þrescan; akin to D. dorschen, OD. derschen, G. dreschen, OHG. dreskan, Icel. þreskja, Sw. tröska, Dan. tærske, Goth. þriskan, Lith. traszketi to rattle, Russ. treskate to burst, crackle, tresk' a crash, OSlav. troska a stroke of lighting. Cf. Thresh.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Thrashed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Thrashing.]

defs: To beat out grain from, as straw or husks; to beat the straw or husk of (grain) with a flail; to beat off, as the kernels of grain; as, to thrash wheat, rye, or oats; to thrash over the old straw.
quotes: The wheat was reaped, thrashed, and winnowed by machines.

defs: To beat soundly, as with a stick or whip; to drub.
To practice thrashing grain or the like; to perform the business of beating grain from straw; as, a man who thrashes well.
Hence, to labor; to toil; also, to move violently.
quotes: I rather would be Maevius, thrash for rhymes, Like his, the scorn and scandal of the times.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Threshed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Threshing.]

quotes: He would thresh, and thereto dike and delve.
